name: 15_gh_pr18_threads_graphql
cmd: gh api graphql -f query='query { repository(owner:"Doogie201", name:"promptops-gui") { pullRequest(number: 18) { number state url reviewThreads(first: 100) { nodes { id isResolved comments(first: 20) { nodes { author { login } body } } } } } } }'
stdout:
{"data":{"repository":{"pullRequest":{"number":18,"state":"MERGED","url":"https://github.com/Doogie201/promptops-gui/pull/18","reviewThreads":{"nodes":[{"id":"PRRT_kwDORZ8p_M5yNkqX","isResolved":true,"comments":{"nodes":[{"author":{"login":"chatgpt-codex-connector"},"body":"**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Detect removed global bindings in drift check**

In `assessSettingsImpactOnPinnedTemplate`, the `required` set is built by filtering placeholders with `hasOwn(nextGlobalBindings, name)`, which means placeholders whose bindings were removed are excluded before `missing` is calculated. In the common settings-edit path, deleting a global binding that a pinned template still depends on will incorrectly return `ok: true`, so incompatible settings changes can slip through and later render/migration steps can proceed with broken placeholder resolution.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎."}]}},{"id":"PRRT_kwDORZ8p_M5yNkqZ","isResolved":true,"comments":{"nodes":[{"author":{"login":"chatgpt-codex-connector"},"body":"**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Reset regex state before unresolved placeholder test**

`evaluateUnresolvedRequirements` calls `.test` on the shared `PLACEHOLDER_PATTERN` regex, which is declared with the `g` flag, so `lastIndex` persists across calls. When previews are compiled repeatedly, this can cause `unresolved_placeholders` to appear/disappear nondeterministically for identical rendered content (notably when only one unresolved token exists), undermining the deterministic preview behavior this module is supposed to enforce.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎."}]}}]}}}}}
